commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are merge conflict resolution, contributor-supplied real behavior proof, and maintainer compatibility acceptance rather than a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff is auth-sensitive and touches a packaging script, but I found no concrete security or supply-chain regression in the patch shape. (sha=1a9fedd20445b22114eaabb57f4044fa0bc9ddfc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `models auth login --force`, auth failover remediation hints, a default-off session-scoped fallback skip cache controlled by `OPENCLAW_FALLBACK_SKIP_TTL_MS`, docs, tests, and adjacent CI-drift fixes.

PR surface: Source +423, Tests +513, Docs +32, Other +177. Total +1145 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a proposed PR change rather than a standalone current-main bug report. Source inspection shows current main lacks the new `models auth login --force` flag and fallback skip cache, while the branch adds them with unit coverage and partial terminal proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are merge conflict resolution, contributor-supplied real behavior proof, and maintainer compatibility acceptance rather than a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(auth): force re-login flag, remediation hint, and session-scoped fallback skip cache
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `openclaw models auth login --force` so operators can drop cached provider auth profiles before starting the existing provider auth flow.
- Add auth-class failover remediation hints that produce shell-safe recovery commands through the shared CLI formatter.
- Add a session-scoped fallback skip cache for non-primary auth failures. It is disabled by default; operators opt in with `OPENCLAW_FALLBACK_SKIP_TTL_MS`, clamped to `[1s, 10m]`.
- Keep web-search provider validation on the shared install-catalog resolver so config validation and setup use one source of truth.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `--force` purges cached provider auth profiles only after a valid auth method is resolved; auth failover summaries include a formatted recovery command; opted-in fallback skip state is keyed by `(sessionId, provider, model)` and causes the second turn to skip the same non-primary auth-failing candidate while preserving the primary attempt.

**Real environment tested:** Local Ubuntu 24.04 / WSL2 checkout, Node v24.15.0, branch `fix/auth-relogin-and-skip-known-bad-fallbacks`. Final head is `6d044f3e14`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/validation.ts src/agents/failover-error.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/config/config.web-search-provider.test.ts docs/gateway/authentication.md docs/concepts/model-failover.md
pnpm exec oxlint src/config/validation.ts src/config/config.web-search-provider.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.test.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.ts src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts -t "uses the opt-in auth skip cache on the second turn" --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
Test Files  6 passed (6)
Tests       284 passed (284)

✓ |agents-core| src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > uses the opt-in auth skip cache on the second turn for the same session
✓ |agents-support| src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > uses the opt-in auth skip cache on the second turn for the same session

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       2 passed | 144 skipped (146)

git diff --check
DIFF_CHECK_EXIT:0

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/validation.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
OXFMT_EXIT:0

pnpm exec oxlint src/config/validation.ts
OXLINT_EXIT:0
```

**Observed result after fix:** The second turn still attempts the primary `openai/gpt-5.4`, skips the previously auth-failing non-primary `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, records a synthetic skipped attempt containing `recent auth failure`, then succeeds on `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`. The final config cleanup removes the parallel web-search install catalog scan from `src/config/validation.ts` and routes validation through the shared catalog resolver.

**What was not tested:** Live OAuth re-auth against a real provider, because that requires interactive credentials.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxlint ...`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] Targeted vitest suite for changed auth/fallback seams
- [x] Focused second-turn fallback skip regression
- [ ] Live OAuth re-auth round trip
target.author: mertbasar0

commands.author: MertBasar0

target.title: fix(auth): force re-login flag, remediation hint, and session-scoped fallback skip cache
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `openclaw models auth login --force` so operators can drop cached provider auth profiles before starting the existing provider auth flow.
- Add auth-class failover remediation hints that produce shell-safe recovery commands through the shared CLI formatter.
- Add a session-scoped fallback skip cache for non-primary auth failures. It is disabled by default; operators opt in with `OPENCLAW_FALLBACK_SKIP_TTL_MS`, clamped to `[1s, 10m]`.
- Keep web-search provider validation on the shared install-catalog resolver so config validation and setup use one source of truth.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `--force` purges cached provider auth profiles only after a valid auth method is resolved; auth failover summaries include a formatted recovery command; opted-in fallback skip state is keyed by `(sessionId, provider, model)` and causes the second turn to skip the same non-primary auth-failing candidate while preserving the primary attempt.

**Real environment tested:** Local Ubuntu 24.04 / WSL2 checkout, Node v24.15.0, branch `fix/auth-relogin-and-skip-known-bad-fallbacks`. Final head is `6d044f3e14`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/validation.ts src/agents/failover-error.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/config/config.web-search-provider.test.ts docs/gateway/authentication.md docs/concepts/model-failover.md
pnpm exec oxlint src/config/validation.ts src/config/config.web-search-provider.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.test.ts src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.ts src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/fallback-skip-cache.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts -t "uses the opt-in auth skip cache on the second turn" --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
Test Files  6 passed (6)
Tests       284 passed (284)

✓ |agents-core| src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > uses the opt-in auth skip cache on the second turn for the same session
✓ |agents-support| src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > uses the opt-in auth skip cache on the second turn for the same session

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       2 passed | 144 skipped (146)

git diff --check
DIFF_CHECK_EXIT:0

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/validation.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
OXFMT_EXIT:0

pnpm exec oxlint src/config/validation.ts
OXLINT_EXIT:0
```

**Observed result after fix:** The second turn still attempts the primary `openai/gpt-5.4`, skips the previously auth-failing non-primary `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, records a synthetic skipped attempt containing `recent auth failure`, then succeeds on `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`. The final config cleanup removes the parallel web-search install catalog scan from `src/config/validation.ts` and routes validation through the shared catalog resolver.

**What was not tested:** Live OAuth re-auth against a real provider, because that requires interactive credentials.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxlint ...`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] Targeted vitest suite for changed auth/fallback seams
